howToCreators.title: How-To for Creators
howToCreators.intro: This guide explains the shortest path to receive MB1-style boost messages from compatible apps without extra implementation detail.
howToCreators.step1Title: 1) Choose and create the right bucket type
howToCreators.step1Body: Create an RSS network bucket for your show organization, then create RSS channel buckets for each feed you want to connect.
howToCreators.step2Title: 2) Add your metaBoost tag to RSS
howToCreators.step2Body: Copy the podcast:metaBoost tag from the Add to RSS tab and add it to your RSS channel so supporting apps know where to send MB1 boosts.
howToCreators.step3Body: Use Verify Metaboost Enabled in the app to confirm the feed tag is live before sharing the bucket.
howToCreators.step4Title: 4) Understand channel vs item message behavior
howToCreators.step4Body: Channel buckets aggregate feed-level boost visibility while RSS item buckets track item-level boosts. Current message pages only display boost messages, not stream telemetry rows.
howToCreators.step5Body: Choose public or private message visibility for your bucket and share private or public links based on your moderation needs.
howToCreators.policyMiddle: for payment and refund disclaimers. Developer integration details are in

howToDevelopers.title: How-To for Developers
howToDevelopers.intro: This guide covers the MB1 integration flow your app needs to send and confirm boost messages with MetaBoost.
howToDevelopers.step1Body: Call the MB1 capability endpoint first and respect message_char_limit, terms_of_service_url, schema_definition_url, and public message links returned by the server.
howToDevelopers.step2Title: 2) Submit boosts with required and optional metadata
howToDevelopers.step2Body: POST to the MB1 boost endpoint with required fields plus optional metadata like amount_unit. Use action='boost' for display-intended messages and action='stream' for telemetry events that are not shown in current message views.
howToDevelopers.step3Body: Use the confirm-payment endpoint after your payment flow completes. Message acceptance is not itself proof that a payment was settled.
howToDevelopers.step4Body: Use public message endpoints for bucket, channel, or item scopes when the creator has enabled public visibility.
howToDevelopers.step5Title: 5) Handle units, limits, and errors safely
howToDevelopers.step5Body: Render BTC + satoshis as satoshis, treat omitted amount_unit as null with no implied unit label, and handle validation/rate-limit errors without retry floods.
howToDevelopers.policyMiddle: for payment/refund disclaimers and responsibilities between sender, creator, and payment service provider. Creator onboarding guidance is in
